arts.ttl: Reading 'in talks' over possibility of extending loan for Wolves star
arts.sum: Reading manager Noel Hunt has confirmed that the club are in talks with Wolverhampton Wanderers loanee Chem Campbell over the possibility of extending his loan deal. The 21-year-old, who has made eight appearances for the Old Gold to date, has nine goal contributions to his name so far this season but is only under contract until January 5. The EFL only cleared his arrival on deadline day due to the expectation that Rob Couhig was to takeover, something which did not happen and is now leading to the 75-year-old filing a lawsuit against the club. A regular since arriving, Campbell has improved throughout the campaign and is regularly one of the top performers for the Royals. Speaking ahead of Saturday's trip to Lincoln City, Hunt explained that discussions are being had over any possible options.

arts.ttl: 'Months rather than days': Reading hit with injury blow ahead of Lincoln City trip
arts.sum: Reading have been hit with a double injury blow ahead of Saturday's trip to Lincoln City, with Ben Elliott and Amadou Mbengue out. The Royals travel up to Sincil Bank looking for a first win under Noel Hunt to kickstart a busy festive period. Mbengue was taken off at half-time of Saturday's 3-0 defeat to Blackpool with a mild concussion, while midfielder Elliott limped off in the closing stages and is now seeing a specialist for a thigh injury. Speaking to the media ahead of the trip to face the Imps, manager Hunt said: "Amadou [Mbengue] had a mild concussion so he will miss the weekend. [Ben] Elliott sustained a decent thigh injury. We’re waiting to see the extent of it- he sees a specialist tomorrow. Probably months rather than days.

arts.ttl: Reading fans invited to remember loved ones in final home match of 2024
arts.sum: Reading's home match with Mansfield Town has been selected for the annual 'Royals Remembered' fixture. Reading Football Club, in close connection with Supporters' Trust at Reading [STAR], are to put together a memorial montage ahead of kick-off on Sunday, December 29 when Mansfield visit the Select Car Leasing Stadium for the first time. A tough year for all connected with the club, Christmas is a time to remember that it is the most important of unimportant things, and there is more to life than the Royals. Last season, 100s of submissions were sent in as the Royals beat Wigan Athletic just days before Christmas, Femi Azeez scoring the winner ahead of what was a prosperous festive period for Ruben Selles' side.

arts.ttl: Brian McDermott takes charge of Reading for first time with Bristol City draw
arts.sum: On this day in 2009, Reading took to the field for the first time under the management of now legendary boss Brian McDermott, drawing 1-1 at Bristol City after Brendan Rodgers' departure. The previous season, Steve Coppell's attempt to lead the club back into the Premier League at the first time of asking failed at the play-off stage, losing in the semi-final to Burnley. Among wholesale changes over the summer of 2009 was the appointment of Brendan Rodgers, a former Royal youth player and reserve team coach who had joined from Watford. Now a multiple title winner with Celtic and Leicester City, his 23-game spell in Berkshire left a lot to be desired and with Reading sat 21st in the Championship, Rodgers was sacked after a 1-1 draw with Scunthorpe United.

arts.ttl: Former Wolves striker 'would love' to see the back of Reading owner Dai Yongge
arts.sum: Former Wolverhampton Wanderers and West Bromwich Albion striker Don Goodman would 'love' for Reading to get a new owner as the Dai Yongge saga stretches into its third year. The Sky Sports pundit infamously scored Walsall's winner against the Royals in the 2001 third tier play-off final, gaining a notorious reputation among supporters. A regular on television for the last 20 years, Goodman is one of the leading voices of the EFL. Keeping a keen eye on proceedings in Berkshire, Reading are heading into 2025 in the same crisis they have endured from 2023, with late payments, player sales, points deductions and transfer embargoes. Experiencing false dawns on multiple occasions since being up for sale officially 18 months ago, the supporters were left dejected once again this month as popular manager Ruben Selles left to join Championship bottom club Hull City.
